title: Troilus and Criseyde
summary: The repository XML is a local copy or version of University of Michigan CME item Troilus, distributed through Oxford Text Archive identifier U-1689-A and keyed from Barry A. Windeatt's 1984 critical edition. Windeatt's edition is eclectic and reports variants from the extant manuscript tradition; it is not a transcription of one manuscript. The electronic text omits the printed edition's front and back matter, editorial notes, bibliography, and parallel Italian Il Filostrato.
review_notes: The aggregate manuscript-tradition entity prevents a false single-witness lineage claim.
U-M's current No Copyright–United States statement, the older OTA personal-scholarly-use notice in the XML, and possible copyright in Windeatt's 1984 editorial contribution are recorded separately.
Morgan MS M.817 is included as an accessible representative witness, not as the sole or stated base manuscript of Windeatt's edition.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for Troilus
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item Troilus
entities.description: Publicly viewable Middle English text derived from Windeatt's edition and associated with OTA U-1689-A.

entities.label: Windeatt 1984 edition of Troilus and Criseyde

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, Troilus & Criseyde: A New Edition of ‘The Book of Troilus,’ ed. Barry A. Windeatt (London and New York: Longman, 1984).
bibliographic.title: Troilus & Criseyde: A New Edition of ‘The Book of Troilus’

notes: The printed edition includes facing Italian Il Filostrato, glosses, notes, variants, and bibliography; these are not all present in the electronic text.
The Cambridge Faculty of English describes it as presenting textual variants of the extant Troilus manuscripts.

entities.label: Extant manuscript tradition of Troilus and Criseyde
entities.description: An aggregate description of the multiple surviving manuscript witnesses collated in modern critical editions; it is deliberately not a manuscript object or a claim of one base witness.
notes: Consult Windeatt's list of manuscripts and apparatus for sigla and reading-level dependencies.

entities.label: Morgan Library & Museum MS M.817
entities.description: A complete early witness of Troilus and Criseyde, securely dated 1403–1413 and probably made in London.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 12044
entities.description: An imperfect fifteenth-century witness of the five-book poem, lacking portions near the end.

scope.notes: Matching identifier, title, source statement, and text support this relationship; exact transfer date is undocumented.

object.description: Windeatt's edited Middle English text only

scope.notes: Front/back matter, editorial notes, bibliography, and parallel Italian text are omitted.

subject.description: Critical Middle English text and apparatus

object.description: Extant witnesses represented in Windeatt's manuscript list and variants

scope.notes: This aggregate edge expressly avoids identifying a single base witness.

access.access_method: Read the checked-out CME submodule path
Library catalogue and commercial second-hand channels
access.format: Print book; limited Google Books record

access.access_method: Morgan catalogue and image viewer for the selected opening leaf

local_copies.label: Morgan selected opening image, MS M.817 fol. 1r

work_portion.label: Opening of Troilus and Criseyde
notes: This is one selected leaf from the work, not the whole work and not the complete 120-leaf manuscript.
Cached only because it is the sole verified public manuscript image; it is explicitly not substituted for a full-codex facsimile.

access.format: Catalogue plus one selected JPEG manuscript image
notes: The public thumbnail viewer exposed only fol. 1r during direct inspection; no complete public 120-leaf facsimile or bulk download was verified.
The ignored local cache is a research copy and does not independently grant redistribution rights.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalogue

rights.basis: Chaucer died in 1400 and the underlying work predates modern copyright by centuries.

rights.component: Windeatt's 1984 selection, edited text, apparatus, glosses, notes, and other original editorial contribution
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1984 and its living editor was born in 1950; no public-domain dedication or open license for the edition was verified.
notes: This does not place Chaucer's underlying text under copyright.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item Troilus
rights.basis: U-M expressly labels the item public domain and applies No Copyright–United States.
notes: This provider assertion conflicts in practical scope with the older OTA restriction preserved in the XML and does not itself explain the status of Windeatt's editorial contribution.

rights.component: The local XML encoding and its use as an OTA-derived file
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The XML header states that OTA U-1689-A was made available for personal scholarly use only, while U-M's current item page supplies a broader public-domain statement.
notes: Resolve the conflicting statements before commercial redistribution of this particular encoding.

rights.component: Modern Morgan manuscript photographs and interface assets
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: Public viewing does not by itself establish permission to redistribute the image files.

editorial_practices.description: The electronic edition retains the poem but omits printed front/back matter, editorial notes, and bibliography.
The edition records textual variants from the extant manuscript tradition rather than transcribing one witness.
editorial_practices.scope: Critical Middle English text and apparatus

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_from_OTA/Troilus.xml, HEADER and full text.
evidence.summary: Identifies OTA U-1689-A, U-M distribution, Windeatt 1984 as source, omissions, creation date 1385, and the five-book text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, ‘Geoffrey Chaucer's Troilus and Criseyde,’ CME item Troilus.
evidence.summary: Supplies title, authorship, 1984 imprint, OTA identifier, public access, NoC-US statement, and five-book contents.

evidence.citation: Indiana University Libraries, Troilus & Criseyde: a new edition of Chaucer's The Book of Troilus, PR1895 .W56 1984.
evidence.summary: Confirms editor Barry A. Windeatt (1950–), Longman 1984 imprint, extent, and ISBN.

evidence.citation: University of Cambridge Faculty of English, ‘Recommended Editions,’ Troilus & Criseyde: Translation & Commentary.
evidence.summary: Describes Windeatt 1984 as including critical notes, extant-manuscript variants, and parallel Il Filostrato.

evidence.citation: Morgan Library & Museum, MS M.817, Troilus and Criseyde.
evidence.summary: Catalogues a 120-leaf parchment witness made probably in London between 1403 and 1413 and links to a selected-image viewer.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the Morgan MS M.817 thumbnail viewer and selected fol. 1r JPEG.
evidence.summary: The public thumbnail route exposed one image, fol. 1r, rather than a complete manuscript sequence. The selected JPEG visibly reproduces the opening leaf and must not be represented as a whole-codex facsimile.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 12044: Geoffrey Chaucer, Troilus and Criseyde.
evidence.summary: Describes an imperfect five-book manuscript witness and its missing portions.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Provides the U.S. duration framework used to distinguish the medieval work from a 1984 editorial contribution.

open_questions.question: What permission or rights determination allowed OTA and U-M to distribute a text keyed from Windeatt 1984, and does it permit commercial redistribution of this XML-derived text?
next_steps: Ask U-M and the Oxford Text Archive for the accession and permission record for U-1689-A.
Do not infer rights in Windeatt's apparatus from Chaucer's public-domain text or U-M's item label.

open_questions.question: Which witnesses and editorial principles determine each reading in Windeatt's base text?
next_steps: Consult Windeatt's full introduction, list of manuscripts, and apparatus; record individual witness edges only where explicitly supported.
